user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator sees a terminal loop or track result only after every required status, Git, supervisor, and lifecycle-event effect for that result is durably recorded.
rationale: Architecture review #108 found terminal labels that can outrun their effects: slice non-PASS persistence errors are discarded, track completion can be recorded before merge succeeds, and single-task supervision defaults to done on later failure. This slice defines terminal-outcome-v1 and makes the real slice and track finalizers fail closed. It addresses #63 and #93 without claiming one cross-store ACID transaction; ordered effects and explicit reconciliation make partial failure visible and recoverable.
in_scope: Define terminal-outcome-v1 as a typed result that distinguishes PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, paused, cancelled, ready_to_merge, and persistence_error and carries the durable references required by contract C-01.
Make RunSlice propagate every terminal status/proof write error instead of returning the intended verdict as though persistence succeeded.
Make single-task supervision release failed on every post-acquire error and done only after the verified Git effect succeeds.
Make track finalization record done only after the track merge/publish effect succeeds; merge or push failure records failed and returns non-zero.
Record one public-safe lifecycle event for each accepted terminal outcome and surface an event-write failure when C-01 requires the event.
Add reconciliation helpers that identify a recorded terminal label whose status revision, Git reference, or event ID is absent or inconsistent.
out_of_scope: Selecting the production task and parallel engines is S02-execution-authority.
Signal propagation and restart recovery are S03-cancellation-recovery.
The versioned command/event service and durable event schema are S04-command-event-service; S01 may use a minimal outcome event adapter until S04 replaces it.
Remote push transport redesign is excluded; this slice makes the existing push result truthful and retains #63 as the source issue.
The twelve planned slices in 2026-07-11-contract-edge-gates, including verdict commit-loop work owned by #93, remain excluded except where S01 consumes their verified contract.

effort_complexity.rationale: The change spans slice, single-task, track, supervisor, Git, and event finalizers, and failure ordering must remain conservative across stores that cannot share one transaction.

risks.description: A new outcome wrapper hides rather than removes a terminal write that still discards an error.
risks.mitigation: Table-driven failure injection covers each terminal effect at the owning RunSlice, Run, and RunTrack integration points, and a source guard rejects discarded terminal persistence calls.

risks.description: A crash between ordered effects leaves an ambiguous partial outcome.
risks.mitigation: Persist intent/effect references before publishing the terminal label and make reconciliation return persistence_error until every required reference can be proven.

risks.description: Treating ready_to_merge as PASS accidentally grants release authority.
risks.mitigation: C-01 keeps readiness distinct from merge success, and command tests assert an attempted automatic merge without delegation exits non-zero.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the verifier returns FAIL or BLOCKED and the corresponding status write fails THE SYSTEM SHALL return persistence_error with a non-zero exit path from RunSlice (C-01, N-01).
WHEN single-task execution fails after supervisor acquisition THE SYSTEM SHALL release the synthetic track as failed (C-01, N-01).
WHEN a track merge or push effect fails THE SYSTEM SHALL retain a failed supervisor state and return a non-PASS TrackResult (C-01, N-01; #63).
WHEN a terminal outcome is accepted THE SYSTEM SHALL return its status revision, Git effect reference when applicable, and lifecycle-event ID in terminal-outcome-v1 (C-01, C-03, C-04, N-01).
IF reconciliation cannot prove any required terminal-outcome-v1 reference THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL classify the outcome as persistence_error (C-01, N-01).
WHEN the real sworn run --parallel command encounters an injected track-merge failure THE SYSTEM SHALL print a non-PASS terminal result and exit non-zero without a done supervisor row (C-01, N-01).
